title: EMD Serono — White Space
html:
EMD Serono — White-Space BD Map

Last updated: 2026-04-10 AO: Rich Durante | AD: Nick Fleischman FY25: $161K | FY26 Target: $160K (flat) | Profitability target: 55%

1. Brand x Service Matrix

Focus: Oncology brands (strongest ZoomRx fit). Fertility and MS brands excluded — low probability, different buyer persona.

Service Bavencio (bladder) Tepmetko (lung) Erbitux (CRC/H&N)
PET ACTIVE (Vinoth) Warm — has project data Cold
ATU Opportunity Opportunity Low fit
HCP-Patient Opportunity Low fit Low fit
MSL Tracking Low fit Low fit Low fit
Digital Tracking Opportunity Opportunity Low fit
Message Testing Opportunity Low fit Low fit
Launch KPI N/A (mature) Opportunity (newer) N/A

Legend: ACTIVE = current project | Opportunity = viable pitch | Low fit = deprioritize | Cold = no relationship/budget signal

2. Top 5 Opportunities (Ranked)

# Opportunity Brand Est. Value Confidence Rationale
1 Bavencio ATU study Bavencio $50-70K High Natural extension of PET relationship. Vinoth can propose directly to Jaya. Urothelial carcinoma landscape shifting with new combos.
2 Bavencio Digital Tracker Bavencio $40-60K Medium Track competitive digital share of voice in bladder cancer. Low lift to pitch alongside PET renewal.
3 Bavencio Message Testing Bavencio $30-50K Medium If Bavencio messaging evolves for maintenance positioning, message testing is a natural fit.
4 Tepmetko PET or Launch KPI Tepmetko $50-80K Low-Medium Tepmetko is a niche MET exon 14 drug. If EMD Serono is investing in data here, existing project data gives us a warm entry. Requires finding the right buyer.
5 Bavencio HCP-Patient study Bavencio $40-60K Low Patient journey in maintenance bladder cancer. Depends on brand team budget and interest.

Total addressable if all convert: ~$210-320K (would meaningfully exceed $160K target) Realistic FY26 upside: $30-70K incremental beyond current Bavencio PET

3. Competitive Context

EMD Serono is the US arm of Merck KGaA — a mid-size pharma player in oncology, dwarfed by Merck & Co, BMS, and Roche in immuno-oncology. Bavencio's maintenance positioning in bladder cancer is defensible but narrow. Competitive pressure comes from Opdivo + Yervoy combinations, Padcev + Keytruda, and emerging ADCs. EMD Serono's insights budget reflects this — they are a focused, cost-conscious buyer. ZoomRx competes here against smaller boutique firms rather than the big-4 consultancies.

4. Connection to AO Plan / Gap Analysis

  • FY26 target is flat at $160K — no growth expectation from Rich or Nick.
  • This is the smallest account in Rich's portfolio. Neither Rich nor Nick will proactively BD into EMD Serono.
  • The entire growth thesis is Vinoth. His ownership of Bavencio PET and direct relationship with Jaya Mugundhan (SC) is the only lever.
  • If Bavencio PET renews ($100-120K) and Vinoth lands one incremental project ($40-60K), the account meets or exceeds target.
  • Risk: If Bavencio PET does NOT renew, the account drops to near zero.

5. Recommended Next Actions for Vinoth

  1. Secure Bavencio PET renewal (Q2 2026). This is non-negotiable. Start renewal conversations with Jaya now. Lock in before budget cycles close.
  2. Pitch Bavencio ATU as a bundled add-on. Position it as a complement to PET — "you track the prescribing, now track the attitudes driving it." Pitch during renewal conversation, not separately.
  3. Explore Tepmetko contact path. Ask Jaya if she has visibility into who runs Tepmetko insights. One warm introduction is worth more than a cold pitch.
  4. Do NOT over-invest BD time here. This account caps at ~$200-250K realistically. Vinoth's time is better spent on Genmab RINA-S or Merck gap-closing. Spend 2-3 hours/month max on EMD Serono BD.
  5. Share one unsolicited Bavencio competitive insight per quarter with Jaya — keeps the relationship warm and positions Vinoth as a thought partner, not just a vendor.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
